rationale: Both candidates correctly confirm the core fix (codes.audio vs legacy audio) matching the GT's main point. X also independently surfaces the GT's key non-blocking concern — that the new containment fallback silently loosens the assertion for ALL callers, not just opt-in ones — backing it with a concrete counterexample, which closely tracks the reviewer's docstring-inconsistency complaint. Y's findings are mostly sweep/validation filler plus low-value nits (missing docstring, 'dead' guard removal, magic numbers, CJK edge case) that never touch the GT's actual concern about the stale strict-behavior docstring, and some claims (downstream info_dict consumption, CJK regex specifics) reference code outside the given diff and are unverifiable from it. Both are concrete and file/line-anchored, but X's comments are tighter, better grounded, and higher-recall against the ground truth.
